_**User (2026-06-26 07:37:39Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: /private/tmp/claude-501/bundled-skills/2.1.193/6d18641b9ea9266effd1022638968d9f/run

**Running means launching the actual app and interacting with it** —
not the test suite, not an `import` of an internal function and a
`console.log`. The app as a user (human or programmatic) would meet
it: the CLI at its command, the server at its socket, the GUI at its
window.

## First: does a project skill already cover this?

A project skill that launches this app is the repo's verified path —
its author already cold-started from a Linux container and committed
what worked: the exact `apt-get` line, the env vars, the patches, the
driver. Use it instead of rediscovering.

```bash
d=$PWD; while :; do
  grep -Hm1 '^description:' "$d"/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null
  [ -e "$d/.git" ] || [ "$d" = / ] && break
  d=$(dirname "$d")
done
```

- **One describes launching/driving this app** → read that SKILL.md
  and follow it verbatim. Don't paraphrase; don't skip the patches.
- **Mega-repo, several plausible, no clear match** → ask the user
  which unit to run.
- **Stale** (fails on mechanics unrelated to your task) → tell the
  user; offer to refresh it via `/run-skill-generator`.
- **Nothing about running** → fall back to the patterns below.

## Otherwise: match the shape, use the pattern

Pick the row closest to your project. Each example walks through
launch + first interaction; ignore any trailing "write the skill"
section — you're using the recipe, not authoring one.

| Project type | Handle | Example |
|---|---|---|
| CLI tool | direct invocation, exit code, stdin/stdout | [examples/cli.md](examples/cli.md) |
| Web server / API | background launch + `curl` smoke | [examples/server.md](examples/server.md) |
| TUI / interactive terminal | tmux `send-keys` / `capture-pane` | [examples/tui.md](examples/tui.md) |
| Electron / desktop GUI | Playwright `_electron` REPL under xvfb | [examples/electron.md](examples/electron.md) |
| Browser-driven | dev server + `chromium-cli` script | [examples/playwright.md](examples/playwright.md) |
| Library / SDK | import-and-call smoke script at the package boundary | [examples/library.md](examples/library.md) |

If nothing fits, start from the closest match and adapt. For a web
app, [examples/playwright.md](examples/playwright.md) — drive it with
`chromium-cli`, no custom driver needed. For a desktop app,
[examples/electron.md](examples/electron.md) — it has the `_electron`
REPL driver skeleton and the tmux wrapping.

## Drive it, don't just launch it

Launching with no interaction proves the entrypoint resolves. That's
not running the app — it's typechecking with extra steps. Drive it to
a point where a user would see something:

- CLI → type a representative command, check the exit code and output.
- Server → hit the route the diff touches with `curl`, read the body.
- TUI → `send-keys` a navigation, `capture-pane` the result.
- GUI → click the button, screenshot the window. **Look at the
  screenshot.** A blank frame is a failure to launch.

If the fallback pattern didn't work out of the box — you had to
install packages, set env vars, patch config, or write a driver —
recommend `/run-skill-generator` in your report so that work gets
captured as a project skill. If it just worked, don't.

1	import React, { useEffect, useRef } from "react";
2	
3	interface Vector {
4	  x: number;
5	  y: number;
6	}
7	
8	class Boid {
9	  position: Vector;
10	  velocity: Vector;
11	  acceleration: Vector;
12	  maxForce: number = 0.2;
13	  maxSpeed: number = 3;
14	  perceptionRadius: number = 50;
15	  index: number = 0;
16	
17	  constructor(x: number, y: number, index: number = 0) {
18	    this.index = index;
19	    this.position = { x, y };
20	    const angle = Math.random() * Math.PI * 2;
21	    this.velocity = {
22	      x: Math.cos(angle),
23	      y: Math.sin(angle),
24	    };
25	    this.acceleration = { x: 0, y: 0 };
26	  }
27	
28	  edges(width: number, height: number) {
29	    if (this.position.x > width) this.position.x = 0;
30	    else if (this.position.x < 0) this.position.x = width;
31	    if (this.position.y > height) this.position.y = 0;
32	    else if (this.position.y < 0) this.position.y = height;
33	  }
34	
35	  applyForce(force: Vector) {
36	    this.acceleration.x += force.x;
37	    this.acceleration.y += force.y;
38	  }
39	
40	  flock(boids: Boid[], mouse: Vector, isMouseDown: boolean) {
41	    const alignment = this.align(boids);
42	    const cohesion = this.cohesion(boids);
43	    const separation = this.separation(boids);
44	    const mouseForce = this.seek(mouse, isMouseDown);
45	
46	    alignment.x *= 1.0;
47	    alignment.y *= 1.0;
48	    cohesion.x *= 1.0;
49	    cohesion.y *= 1.0;
50	    separation.x *= 2.5;
51	    separation.y *= 2.5;
52	
53	    // Mouse force weight can be adjusted
54	    mouseForce.x *= 2.0;
55	    mouseForce.y *= 2.0;
56	
57	    this.applyForce(alignment);
58	    this.applyForce(cohesion);
59	    this.applyForce(separation);
60	    this.applyForce(mouseForce);
61	  }
62	
63	  seek(target: Vector, attract: boolean): Vector {
64	    const desired = {
65	      x: target.x - this.position.x,
66	      y: target.y - this.position.y,
67	    };
68	    const distance = Math.sqrt(desired.x ** 2 + desired.y ** 2);
69	
70	    // Repel radius (flee) vs Attract radius (seek)
71	    // Let's use 400 for both for now
72	    if (distance > 0 && distance < 400) {
73	      // If we want to repel (scare), we reverse the desired vector
74	      if (!attract) {
75	        desired.x *= -1;
76	        desired.y *= -1;
77	      }
78	
79	      const speed = this.maxSpeed;
80	      desired.x = (desired.x / distance) * speed;
81	      desired.y = (desired.y / distance) * speed;
82	
83	      const steer = {
84	        x: desired.x - this.velocity.x,
85	        y: desired.y - this.velocity.y,
86	      };
87	
88	      const steerLen = Math.sqrt(steer.x ** 2 + steer.y ** 2);
89	      if (steerLen > this.maxForce) {
90	        steer.x = (steer.x / steerLen) * this.maxForce;
91	        steer.y = (steer.y / steerLen) * this.maxForce;
92	      }
93	      return steer;
94	    }
95	    return { x: 0, y: 0 };
96	  }
97	
98	  align(boids: Boid[]): Vector {
99	    const steering = { x: 0, y: 0 };
100	    let total = 0;
101	    for (const other of boids) {
102	      const d = Math.sqrt(
103	        (this.position.x - other.position.x) ** 2 +
104	          (this.position.y - other.position.y) ** 2
105	      );
106	      if (other !== this && d < this.perceptionRadius) {
107	        steering.x += other.velocity.x;
108	        steering.y += other.velocity.y;
109	        total++;
110	      }
111	    }
112	    if (total > 0) {
113	      steering.x /= total;
114	      steering.y /= total;
115	      const mag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
116	      if (mag > 0) {
117	        steering.x = (steering.x / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
118	        steering.y = (steering.y / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
119	      }
120	      steering.x -= this.velocity.x;
121	      steering.y -= this.velocity.y;
122	      const steerMag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
123	      if (steerMag > this.maxForce) {
124	        steering.x = (steering.x / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
125	        steering.y = (steering.y / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
126	      }
127	    }
128	    return steering;
129	  }
130	
131	  separation(boids: Boid[]): Vector {
132	    const steering = { x: 0, y: 0 };
133	    let total = 0;
134	    const separationDistance = 30;
135	    for (const other of boids) {
136	      const d = Math.sqrt(
137	        (this.position.x - other.position.x) ** 2 +
138	          (this.position.y - other.position.y) ** 2
139	      );
140	      if (other !== this && d < separationDistance) {
141	        const diff = {
142	          x: this.position.x - other.position.x,
143	          y: this.position.y - other.position.y,
144	        };
145	        diff.x /= d;
146	        diff.y /= d;
147	        steering.x += diff.x;
148	        steering.y += diff.y;
149	        total++;
150	      }
151	    }
152	    if (total > 0) {
153	      steering.x /= total;
154	      steering.y /= total;
155	      const mag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
156	      if (mag > 0) {
157	        steering.x = (steering.x / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
158	        steering.y = (steering.y / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
159	      }
160	      steering.x -= this.velocity.x;
161	      steering.y -= this.velocity.y;
162	      const steerMag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
163	      if (steerMag > this.maxForce) {
164	        steering.x = (steering.x / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
165	        steering.y = (steering.y / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
166	      }
167	    }
168	    return steering;
169	  }
170	
171	  cohesion(boids: Boid[]): Vector {
172	    const steering = { x: 0, y: 0 };
173	    let total = 0;
174	    for (const other of boids) {
175	      const d = Math.sqrt(
176	        (this.position.x - other.position.x) ** 2 +
177	          (this.position.y - other.position.y) ** 2
178	      );
179	      if (other !== this && d < this.perceptionRadius) {
180	        steering.x += other.position.x;
181	        steering.y += other.position.y;
182	        total++;
183	      }
184	    }
185	    if (total > 0) {
186	      steering.x /= total;
187	      steering.y /= total;
188	      steering.x -= this.position.x;
189	      steering.y -= this.position.y;
190	      const mag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
191	      if (mag > 0) {
192	        steering.x = (steering.x / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
193	        steering.y = (steering.y / mag) * this.maxSpeed;
194	      }
195	      steering.x -= this.velocity.x;
196	      steering.y -= this.velocity.y;
197	      const steerMag = Math.sqrt(steering.x ** 2 + steering.y ** 2);
198	      if (steerMag > this.maxForce) {
199	        steering.x = (steering.x / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
200	        steering.y = (steering.y / steerMag) * this.maxForce;
201	      }
202	    }
203	    return steering;
204	  }
205	
206	  update(dt: number) {
207	    this.position.x += this.velocity.x * dt;
208	    this.position.y += this.velocity.y * dt;
209	    this.velocity.x += this.acceleration.x * dt;
210	    this.velocity.y += this.acceleration.y * dt;
211	
212	    const speed = Math.sqrt(this.velocity.x ** 2 + this.velocity.y ** 2);
213	    if (speed > this.maxSpeed) {
214	      this.velocity.x = (this.velocity.x / speed) * this.maxSpeed;
215	      this.velocity.y = (this.velocity.y / speed) * this.maxSpeed;
216	    }
217	
218	    this.acceleration = { x: 0, y: 0 };
219	  }
220	
221	  draw(ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D) {
222	    const angle = Math.atan2(this.velocity.y, this.velocity.x);
223	    // ~18% of the flock fly in brand green, the rest stay black
224	    const isGreen = (this.index * 37) % 100 < 18;
225	    ctx.save();
226	    ctx.translate(this.position.x, this.position.y);
227	    ctx.rotate(angle);
228	    ctx.beginPath();
229	    ctx.moveTo(8, 0);
230	    ctx.lineTo(-4, 4);
231	    ctx.lineTo(-4, -4);
232	    ctx.closePath();
233	    ctx.fillStyle = isGreen ? "rgba(31, 102, 38, 0.72)" : "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6)";
234	    ctx.fill();
235	    ctx.restore();
236	  }
237	}
238	
239	const BoidsBackground: React.FC = () => {
240	  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
241	  const boidsRef = useRef<Boid[]>([]);
242	  const mouseRef = useRef<Vector>({ x: -1000, y: -1000 });
243	  const mouseDownRef = useRef<boolean>(false);
244	
245	  useEffect(() => {
246	    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
247	    if (!canvas) return;
248	
249	    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
250	    if (!ctx) return;
251	
252	    const handleResize = () => {
253	      canvas.width = window.innerWidth;
254	      canvas.height = window.innerHeight;
255	
256	      if (boidsRef.current.length === 0) {
257	        for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
258	          boidsRef.current.push(
259	            new Boid(
260	              Math.random() * canvas.width,
261	              Math.random() * canvas.height,
262	              i
263	            )
264	          );
265	        }
266	      }
267	    };
268	
269	    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
270	      mouseRef.current = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY };
271	    };
272	
273	    const handleMouseDown = () => {
274	      mouseDownRef.current = true;
275	    };
276	
277	    const handleMouseUp = () => {
278	      mouseDownRef.current = false;
279	    };
280	
281	    window.addEventListener("resize", handleResize);
282	    window.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
283	    window.addEventListener("mousedown", handleMouseDown);
284	    window.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
285	    handleResize();
286	
287	    let animationFrameId: number;
288	    let lastTime = performance.now();
289	
290	    const render = (now: number) => {
291	      const dt = Math.min((now - lastTime) / (1000 / 60), 3);
292	      lastTime = now;
293	
294	      ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
295	
296	      for (const boid of boidsRef.current) {
297	        boid.edges(canvas.width, canvas.height);
298	        boid.flock(boidsRef.current, mouseRef.current, mouseDownRef.current);
299	        boid.update(dt);
300	        boid.draw(ctx);
301	      }
302	
303	      animationFrameId = requestAnimationFrame(render);
304	    };
305	
306	    animationFrameId = requestAnimationFrame(render);
307	
308	    return () => {
309	      window.removeEventListener("resize", handleResize);
310	      window.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
311	      window.removeEventListener("mousedown", handleMouseDown);
312	      window.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
313	      cancelAnimationFrame(animationFrameId);
314	    };
315	  }, []);
316	
317	  return (
318	    <canvas
319	      ref={canvasRef}
320	      style={{
321	        position: "fixed",
322	        top: 0,
323	        left: 0,
324	        width: "100%",
325	        height: "100%",
326	        zIndex: 0,
327	        background: "#ffffff",
328	      }}
329	    />
330	  );
331	};
332	
333	export default BoidsBackground;

57	    cursorVisible.current = true;
58	    toggleCursorVisibility();
59	    endX.current = e.pageX;
60	    endY.current = e.pageY;
61	    if (cursorDot.current) {
62	      cursorDot.current.style.top = endY.current + "px";
63	      cursorDot.current.style.left = endX.current + "px";
64	    }
65	  }
66	
67	  function toggleCursorVisibility() {
68	    if (cursorVisible.current) {
69	      if (cursorDot.current) cursorDot.current.style.opacity = "1";
70	      if (cursorDotOutline.current) cursorDotOutline.current.style.opacity = "1";
71	    } else {
72	      if (cursorDot.current) cursorDot.current.style.opacity = "0";
73	      if (cursorDotOutline.current) cursorDotOutline.current.style.opacity = "0";
74	    }
75	  }
76	
77	  function toggleCursorSize() {
78	    if (cursorEnlarged.current) {
79	      if (cursorDot.current)
80	        cursorDot.current.style.transform = "translate(-50%, -50%) scale(0.7)";
81	      if (cursorDotOutline.current)
82	        cursorDotOutline.current.style.transform = "translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1.67)";
83	    } else {
84	      if (cursorDot.current)
85	        cursorDot.current.style.transform = "translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1)";
86	      if (cursorDotOutline.current)
87	        cursorDotOutline.current.style.transform = "translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1)";
88	    }
89	  }
90	
91	  function handleLinks() {
92	    document.querySelectorAll("a").forEach((el) => {
93	      el.addEventListener("mouseover", () => {
94	        (el as HTMLElement).style.color = "#1f6626";
95	      });
96	      el.addEventListener("mouseout", () => {
97	        (el as HTMLElement).style.color = "";
98	      });
99	    });
100	  }
101	
102	  const animateDotOutline = (time: number) => {
103	    if (previousTimeRef.current !== undefined) {
104	      mousePosition.x += (endX.current - mousePosition.x) / 8;
105	      mousePosition.y += (endY.current - mousePosition.y) / 8;
106	      if (cursorDotOutline.current) {
107	        cursorDotOutline.current.style.top = mousePosition.y + "px";
108	        cursorDotOutline.current.style.left = mousePosition.x + "px";
109	      }
110	    }
111	    previousTimeRef.current = time;
112	    requestRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(animateDotOutline);
113	  };
114	
115	  return (
116	    <>
117	      <div ref={cursorDotOutline} id="cursor-dot-outline" />
118	      <div ref={cursorDot} id="cursor-dot" />
119	    </>
120	  );
121	}
122	
123	export default Cursor;
124
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:55:23Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:55:25Z)**_

Found the culprit. The `Cursor` component uses `position: absolute` with `pageX/pageY` values — when the cursor is near the right edge, those elements extend the document width and trigger horizontal scroll. Fix is to make them `position: fixed` and switch to `clientX/clientY`.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:55:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:55:33Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:55:36Z)**_

The root cause: `#cursor-dot` and `#cursor-dot-outline` were `position: absolute`, so setting their `left` to a large `pageX` value physically widened the document and created a scrollable area. Switching to `position: fixed` takes them out of the layout flow entirely (fixed elements never affect scroll width), and `clientX/Y` keeps them tracking the viewport position correctly.

23	function LogoMarquee() {
24	  // Duplicate the list so the track can loop seamlessly.
25	  const track = [...logos, ...logos];
26	
27	  return (
28	    <section
29	      className="marquee"
30	      aria-label="Where our team has studied and worked"
31	    >
32	      <p className="marquee-label">Built by a team from</p>
33	      <div className="marquee-viewport">
34	        <div className="marquee-track">
35	          {track.map((logo, i) => {
36	            // second copy of the list is a visual duplicate for the seamless
37	            // loop — hide it from crawlers/screen readers to avoid double counts
38	            const isDuplicate = i >= logos.length;
39	            return (
40	              <div className="marquee-item" key={i} aria-hidden={isDuplicate}>
41	                <img
42	                  src={logo.src}
43	                  alt={isDuplicate ? "" : logo.alt}
44	                  className="marquee-logo"
45	                  style={{
46	                    height: `calc(${logo.height}px * var(--marquee-scale, 1))`,
47	                  }}
48	                />
49	                {logo.label && (
50	                  <span className="marquee-wordmark">{logo.label}</span>
51	                )}
52	              </div>
53	            );
54	          })}
55	        </div>
56	      </div>
57	    </section>
58	  );
59	}

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:15Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:17Z)**_

The slight leftward scroll is likely the `content::before` glow pseudo-element — it has `inset: -60px` which bleeds 60px beyond the content card edges. On screen widths just above the 768px breakpoint, the left bleed can push past the viewport edge. The most reliable fix is also to clamp `html` itself, since `body` overflow-x alone doesn't always prevent scroll on iOS/mobile browsers.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:19Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:21Z)**_

Also tighten the `content::before` bleed so it can't escape the card on any screen size:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /Users/edmondniu/Documents/Atlia/code/website/src/App.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 07:57:29Z)**_

Two changes: `inset: -60px 0` keeps the vertical bleed (top/bottom glow) but zeroes out the horizontal bleed so the pseudo-element never extends beyond the card's left/right edges. And `overflow-x: hidden` on `html` is the definitive cross-browser fix — mobile Safari in particular ignores `body`-only overflow clamps and requires it on the `html` element.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:04:26Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:04:28Z)**_

The culprit is `overflow-x: hidden` on `html`. When `html` has any overflow property, some browsers (especially Safari) promote it into its own independent scroll container — so you end up with `html` and `body` both wanting to scroll vertically. The other protections on `body` and `#root` are enough; `html` doesn't need it.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:04:29Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /Users/edmondniu/Documents/Atlia/code/website/src/index.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:04:32Z)**_

`body` and `#root` already have `overflow-x: hidden`, which is sufficient to block horizontal bleed. Removing it from `html` collapses back to a single scroll container.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:06:43Z)**_

Found it. `#root` has `overflow-x: hidden`, and per the CSS spec, when you set `overflow-x: hidden` on an element whose `overflow-y` is `visible`, the browser silently promotes `overflow-y` to `auto` — making `#root` its own scroll container alongside `body`. That's your two scrollbars. `body` already handles the clamp; `#root` doesn't need it.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-26 08:06:47Z)**_

The CSS spec has a quirk: if `overflow-x` is `hidden` and `overflow-y` is `visible` (the default), the browser upgrades `overflow-y` to `auto` automatically. That turned `#root` into a silent scroll container sitting inside `body`'s scroll container — two vertical scrollbars. Removing `overflow-x: hidden` from `#root` leaves `body` as the single scroll container.
